column1: # say-it community pronunciation dictionary ‚ÄĒ General American only.
#   2. ipa                primary GenAm IPA, with stress marks (for display + future SSML backends)
#   3. respelling_us      English-like phonetic respelling, fed verbatim to macOS `say` (the path that actually works today)
#   5. alt_respelling_us  `|`-separated alternative respellings, paired with alt_ipa by index, or empty
#   6. source_url         best citation: documented pronunciation page, official FAQ, Wikipedia anchor, etc. Leave empty if unverified.
#   7. source_label       human-readable source label (e.g. "GNU Project official", "Wikipedia ¬ß Pronunciation")
#   8. category           project | product | cli-tool | tool | cs-term | acronym | abbreviation
#                         Only use `creator-clarified` when source_url documents a creator quote / official FAQ entry.
# Why English respelling rather than IPA/PHON phonemes?
#   macOS `say` (2026 era) does not parse `[[inpt PHON]]` markup or SSML `<phoneme>` tags ‚ÄĒ both are read as literal
#   text. So we feed plain English-like spellings that the TTS's internal letter-to-sound rules render acceptably.
#   The `ipa` column is retained for display, for future cloud TTS, and for the Windows SAPI backend (which DOES
#   - All entries are best-effort and welcome PRs to improve. Confidence should reflect what the source URL actually
#     proves, not what you believe to be true. When in doubt, mark `community-consensus` and let a future PR upgrade

column10: K8s community readings vary widely. "koob-control" is heard from Kelsey Hightower and many maintainers; "cube-cuddle" is the running meme; some say it letter-by-letter.
engine-x is the documented reading from the official site.
Wilhite (creator): "It's pronounced JIF, not GIF." Community remains split.
Crockford uses "JAY-son"; "JEE-son" is also widespread. Wikipedia notes the variance.
GOOEY is mainstream (matches Mandarin śēÖśĄŹ sound); "G-U-I" letter-by-letter is RMS/old-school; "gwee" is rare.
One syllable, hard G, rhymes with "new". Page is a dedicated pronunciation FAQ.
kates (8 letters between K and s) ‚ÄĒ community shorthand.
gra-FAH-na is the most common community reading.
KAY-native ‚ÄĒ the K is voiced. (Heard from project maintainers in talks; needs a citable source.)
RED-iss. (Heard from Salvatore Sanfilippo in interviews; citable source TBD.)
Linus's own pronunciation is "LIN-ux" (short I, schwa). Wikipedia documents the variants.
DEB-ee-uhn ‚ÄĒ portmanteau of Debra + Ian Murdock, documented on the about page.
FAQ documents "JANG-go" ‚ÄĒ silent D, like jazz guitarist Django Reinhardt.
view ‚ÄĒ one syllable. (Heard from Evan You in talks; needs a citable source.)
veet ‚ÄĒ French for "quick". (Heard from Evan You in talks; needs a citable source.)
pie-DAN-tic. (Heard from Samuel Colvin in podcasts; needs a citable source.)
Lamport: "lay-tek" (or "lah-tek"); never "lay-teks". Documented on the about page.
Knuth: like "tech" ‚ÄĒ the X is a Greek chi.
char (rhymes with "car") in C/C++ camp; "care" and "kar" also used.
JOO-pi-tur ‚ÄĒ named after Jupiter (planet) + Julia, Python, R.
One syllable, rhymes with "fraud". Anthropic's LLM family.
jacks, one syllable. Google's autograd library.
QUADRANT ‚ÄĒ common reading; project FAQ TBD as a citable source.
quick ‚ÄĒ yes, intentional. Heard from creator Misko Hevery.
shad-C-N ‚ÄĒ common community reading; creator clarification TBD as a citable source.
German for "state": "TSOO-shtund" in German; most English speakers say "ZOO-stund". Creator clarification TBD.
NEE-oh-vim is the most common community reading.
Rich Hickey has said it sounds like "closure". Community reading.
LOO-ah ‚ÄĒ Portuguese for "moon". Documented on the about page.

column1: # --- AI / ML acronyms and models commonly mispronounced ---

column10: LLAH-mah ‚ÄĒ same as the animal. Not letter-by-letter.
DOLL-ee (portmanteau of Dal√≠ + WALL-E); some say it letter-by-letter.
CO-decks ‚ÄĒ same as the manuscript term.
WOZ-um is most common; "WAHS-um" also heard.
G-R-P-C letter-by-letter. (Some say "gee-R-P-C" stressing initial g.)
OH-awth most common; "O-auth" letter-by-O-then-auth also heard.
jot (RFC pronunciation per Section 1) ‚ÄĒ but letter-by-letter "J-W-T" dominates in spoken practice.
cider ‚ÄĒ like the apple drink. Documented in CIDR specs.
gleem ‚ÄĒ one syllable, like the verb.
rock ‚ÄĒ one syllable, named after the mythical bird.
plad ‚ÄĒ one syllable, like the fabric.
mar-EE-mo ‚ÄĒ Japanese for the moss balls.
DAP-er ‚ÄĒ like the adjective dapper. Distributed Application Runtime.
YAY-ger ‚ÄĒ German pronunciation; some say "JAY-ger" too.

column1: # --- v2.0 expansion: AI agents, ML/data, frameworks, CS concepts ---

column10: SMOL-agents ‚ÄĒ Hugging Face tiny agent lib.
FYE-data ‚ÄĒ agent framework now rebranded as Agno.
pie-DAN-tick A-I ‚ÄĒ same maker as Pydantic.
kok ‚ÄĒ proof assistant. Now renamed Rocq.

# --- v2.2 wave 3: edge runtimes, JVM, .NET, Rust web, static-site gens ---

column10: GOD-oh per project FAQ. "GOD-ot" also widespread.
quartz ‚ÄĒ static site gen for digital gardens.
Nous Research's open-source LLM series. Greek messenger god ‚ÄĒ "HER-meez."
AI poll-simulation startup. Egyptian afterlife "field of reeds" ‚ÄĒ "AH-roo."
Literary figure of speech, three syllables "SIM-uh-lee." Frequently mispronounced "sigh-MILE."
Elon Musk's AI company. Spell out the letters: "X-A-I." Not "zai."
AI company behind Pi. Standard English noun: "in-FLEK-shun."
AI agents company. "uh-DEPT" ‚ÄĒ stress on second syllable.
KAIR-ik-ter dot A-I. Letters at the end.
per-PLEK-suh-tee ‚ÄĒ same as the English noun.
Transformer ASIC chip startup. Past tense of "etch" ‚ÄĒ single syllable.
Greek philosophical "intellect." Modern English "NOWSS" (rhymes with house); classical "NOOS."
Long-context code-model lab. Same as the English word + dev.
AI coding assistant. Stress on second syllable.
Memory layer for AI agents. "mem-ZERO" ‚ÄĒ the digit zero.
LPU inference chip company. Filed trademark before xAI's Grok ‚ÄĒ both read "GRAHK."
Paid privacy search engine. Japanese for "key" ‚ÄĒ "KAH-gee" (hard g).
Alibaba's open LLM family. Single syllable "KWEN" ‚ÄĒ short for ťÄöšĻČŚćÉťóģ.
ByteDance chatbot ŤĪÜŚĆÖ. Pinyin d√≤u-bńĀo ‚Üí "doh-BOW" (bow as in bow-and-arrow).
Shanghai AI lab. Same as the game-theory term.
Chinese LLM lab ÁôĺŚ∑Ě. Pinyin b«éi-chuńĀn ‚Üí "BYE-chwahn."
Google's open-weights model. "JEM-uh" ‚ÄĒ distinct from Gemini.
Microsoft's small-model series. Greek letter ŌÜ ‚ÄĒ US "FAI"; British/math "FEE."
Cohere's retrieval LLM. "Command R" ‚ÄĒ R as a letter.
TII's open LLM. Same as the bird.
xAI's chatbot. Heinlein coined word ‚ÄĒ "GRAHK." Homophone of chip company Groq.
Generative video startup. Same as the English word.
Node-based UI for Stable Diffusion. "KUM-fee U-I" (letters for UI).
ByteDance's AI code IDE. Single syllable "TRAY."
AI presentation builder. "TOHM" ‚ÄĒ rhymes with home.
AI deck/site builder. Greek letter ő≥ ‚ÄĒ "GAM-uh."
AI audio/video editor. "dih-SKRIPT" ‚ÄĒ stress on second syllable.
Friendly Interactive SHell. Same as the animal.
Shell history manager. Aztec turtle deity ‚ÄĒ "AY-too-in" common.
Meta's VCS. Same as the young tree.
Mixture of Experts. Spelled "M-O-E" in papers; some pronounce it "MOH" like the Three Stooges character.
Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback. Always spelled out.
Letters "S-S-L". Deprecated in favor of TLS but term persists.
IETF + Google docs: "quick". From "Quick UDP Internet Connections".
cores; rhymes with "doors". Cross-Origin Resource Sharing.
Letter-by-letter dominant; Tim Berners-Lee uses "earl" but it never stuck.
oh-AUTH two. Same prefix as OAuth.
YOH-loh ‚ÄĒ You Only Look Once. Same as the slang.
Toeverything launches with "a-FINE"; math/CS reading "AF-fine" also heard.
WAG-mee. "We are all gonna make it" ‚ÄĒ React hooks for Ethereum.
Puerto Rican coqu√≠ frog; "KO-kee". Open TTS.
aitch-tee-tee-pie ‚ÄĒ like the suffix "-ie" in English.
HOP-skotch; the playground game. API client.
DEE-fye; do + ify. LLM app platform.
Dutch: "DIKE-strah". Edsger himself corrected English speakers many times.
Knuth (creator): "tree" (from "retrieval"). Most engineers say "try" to disambiguate from binary tree.
dag common in pipeline tools (Airflow, dagster); letter-by-letter "D-A-G" in CS papers.
acid ‚ÄĒ the word. Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability.
base ‚ÄĒ the word. Basically Available, Soft state, Eventual consistency.
cap ‚ÄĒ the word. CAP theorem: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance.
REP-uhl common in Lisp/Clojure; letter-by-letter common elsewhere. Read-Eval-Print Loop.
LIE-foh common in US; "LEE-foh" also heard. Last In First Out.
Letters "A-O-T". Ahead-of-Time compilation. Counterpart to JIT.
nat the word common; "N-A-T" also heard. Network Address Translation.
wan rhymes with "pan". Wide Area Network.
lan rhymes with "pan". Local Area Network.
arp the word. Address Resolution Protocol.
oh-WASP. Open Worldwide Application Security Project.
S-bom common; letter-by-letter also heard. Software Bill of Materials.
R-back common in Kubernetes; letter-by-letter in enterprise. Role-Based Access Control.
waff common; rhymes with "Jeff". Web Application Firewall.
seem ‚ÄĒ like the word. Security Information and Event Management.
HIP-uh. One P only in pronunciation. Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act.
nonce ‚ÄĒ single syllable. "Number used Once". Common in crypto.
simd one syllable common; letter-by-letter also heard. Single Instruction Multiple Data.
Letter-by-letter dominant; "envy-M-E" rare. Non-Volatile Memory Express.
raid the word. Redundant Array of Independent Disks.
Letters "E-L-T". Extract, Load, Transform. Successor to ETL.
gan the word common; letters in formal contexts. Generative Adversarial Network.
RAY-loo common in US; "REH-loo" also heard. Rectified Linear Unit.
JELL-oo common; "G-E-L-U" also heard. Gaussian Error Linear Unit.
rope the word. Rotary Position Embedding.
tanch rhymes with "ranch" common; "T-A-N-H" letters also heard. Hyperbolic tangent.
face common (FB AI Similarity Search); "fice" also heard.
John Ousterhout (creator): "tickle". Official Tcl Developer Xchange confirms.
auk ‚ÄĒ like the seabird, rhymes with "hawk". Named after creators' initials but pronounced as a word.
go the word; "Golang" common in writing/SEO but pronounced "Go".
Letters "N-P-M". Officially lowercase, not an acronym.
Letter-by-letter dominant; "spa" the word also heard. Single-Page Application.
dom rhymes with "mom". Document Object Model.
AD-uhm W ‚ÄĒ Adam with decoupled Weight decay.
trans-FOR-mer ‚ÄĒ the architecture, not the toy.
burt ‚ÄĒ like the name. Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers.
Google brand: "pom" (palm tree); letter-by-letter also heard. Pathways Language Model.
clip the word. Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training.
col-BURT ‚ÄĒ like Stephen Colbert. Contextualized Late interaction over BERT.
GUN-uh-corn ‚ÄĒ like a unicorn with a gun. Green Unicorn.
WIZ-gee common; letter-by-letter also heard. Web Server Gateway Interface.
Letters dominant; "oom" rare. Out Of Memory.
